title: Swinney to 'explore' banning US flights from Prestwick

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: First Minister John Swinney says he could ban US military flights from using Prestwick Airport if they are linked to offensive operations against…

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: First Minister John Swinney speaking as he attends the opening of the Techscaler hub at Water's Edge, Dundee. (Image: Jane Barlow/PA Wire) This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: John Swinney has said he would consider banning US military flights from using Prestwick if it emerged that the airport was being used to support offensive military operations in Iran. Speaking to the BBC’s Laura Kuenssberg , the First Minister said he was “seeking clarity from the United Kingdom Government”.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Swinney considering ban on US military flights using Prestwick
